ip_outcome_note: Card name and frame subtitle both read 'Marnie'; PokemonTCG.io API confirms card ID swsh1-169 with name 'Marnie'; the figure's distinctive pink-and-black dress and asymmetric pigtail hairstyle match canonical Marnie art. No ambiguity.

trivia_bullets.text: Marnie is a rival character in Pokemon Sword & Shield (2019) who specializes in Dark-type Pokemon. She aims to become Champion to revitalize her hometown Spikemuth — a dilapidated city without its own Power Spot. She eventually succeeds her brother Piers as Spikemuth's Gym Leader after the player becomes Champion. [Bulbapedia: Marnie, via WebSearch snippet]

The Sword & Shield base set (set ID swsh1) released February 7, 2020, with 202 printed cards. Marnie appears as card no. 169/202 (Holo Rare) and also has a Full Art (no. 200/202) and Secret Rainbow Rare (no. 208/202) in the same set. Artist on this print is kirisAki. [PokemonTCG.io API swsh1-169]

Marnie is a hand-disruption Supporter: both players shuffle their hands to the bottom of their deck; the user draws 5, the opponent draws 4. It was considered a dual-purpose card — consistency engine AND disruption — and was frequently paired with Professor's Research and Boss's Orders in competitive Sword & Shield-era aggro decks. It is legal in Unlimited and Expanded formats but rotated out of Standard. [PokemonTCG.io API swsh1-169; suprpetrix.com competitive guide via WebSearch snippet]
sources: suprpetrix.com Pokemon TCG supporter guide (via WebSearch snippet)

trivia_bullets.text: The Marnie Supporter card was reprinted extensively across the Sword & Shield era: a non-holofoil in the Build & Battle Box, a Holofoil reprint in Champion's Path, and multiple Japanese-exclusive reprints in VMAX Starter Sets, VMAX Climax, and others. [Bulbapedia: Marnie (Sword & Shield 169) via WebSearch snippet]
sources: Serebii.net TCG: Marnie card listings (via WebSearch snippet)
